section_title.title:
Not an engineer?
You can still help.
section_title.subtitle: Support the Mission
section_title.description: If mentoring isn't the right fit, you can support VWC by donating, hiring our graduates, or spreading the word. Every contribution — time, money, or a referral — gets a veteran closer to their first engineering role.

items.title: Decide to Mentor
items.description: You're a working engineer with production experience. You've got an hour or two a week. You want that time to mean something. That's enough to start.

items.title: Submit the Mentor Form
items.description: Fill out the registration form below with your GitHub or LinkedIn, technical expertise, and availability. Takes five minutes.

items.title: Interview With the VWC Team
items.description: A short conversation to understand your technical strengths, schedule, and what you want to get out of mentoring. We're checking for alignment, not gatekeeping.

items.title: Confirm Your Availability
items.description: We'll align your schedule with the current cohort timeline. Mentorship runs alongside our 17-week program — we need to know you can commit for the duration.

items.title: Get Matched
items.description: We pair you with a veteran based on your technical domain, experience level, and availability. The goal is a close enough gap that your guidance is practical and current, not theoretical.

items.title: Onboard
items.description: You'll get access to our Slack, the cohort's progress tracking, and context on your mentee's background and goals. You'll know exactly where they are in the Hashflag Stack before your first call.

items.title: Mentor
items.description: Weekly 1-on-1s. Code reviews on their pull requests. Career strategy as they approach the job search. You're their engineering manager for the duration — the person who tells them the truth about their code and their readiness.

items.title: Stay Connected
items.description: Most mentors stay in the VWC Slack long after their mentee graduates. Some come back for another cohort. Some end up hiring a VWC graduate at their own company. The relationship doesn't expire when the program ends.
